About

I'm a software engineer based near Bristol, UK.

I started my career in the early 2000s as a technology journalist. As traditional media was challenged by new media, I moved into online journalism and later started my own consultancy practice, providing website development and digital content production services.

My technical skills matured in lockstep with the advancement of the World Wide Web and JavaScript ecosystems. Today, I'm a self-taught software engineer working for BJSS, a global technology consultancy, where I build "single-page" web applications for companies like SpecSavers.

My blog is a commentary on emerging technology and trends across the whole IT industry, but with a focus on my area of domain expertise: the web platform.
